The leader account is a newly created account, which refers to the founder of the club. The leader account does not allow betting, has the highest authority of the club, and cannot be modified or transferred once created.

What is the Club Guarantee? Because third-party games (including WG Sports) need to pay bills in proportion on a regular basis. In order to prevent bad debts or malicious consumption, the club needs to pay a certain deposit before it can be opened. The deposit determines the bill amount and the amount of third-party games that can be brought in (except for WG native games and club games, which are not subject to the limit). Therefore, the more deposits paid, the greater the amount that can be brought into third-party games; and the corresponding deposit will be automatically deducted after the bill is settled, so a deposit must be recharged from time to time to ensure the normal operation of the club. Calculation formula: Club deposit = unsettled bills + (three parties that can be brought in + three parties that have been brought in) / 10.

Example: A club has paid a deposit of 5,000, and the current unsettled bill is 1,000. The ratio of the deposit and the third-party game brought in is 10. Then the third-party game has been brought in and the maximum amount that can be brought into the third-party game is: (5000-1000)*10 = 40000.
Refers to whether to display the club's registration entrance to the outside world (whether to allow external personnel to register), and all registrations do not need to be reviewed.

The amount of third-party games that can be brought in refers to the total amount of third-party games that can be brought into the future by members of the club who have not entered third-party games (including WG Sports) (excluding WG native games and club games), that is, the available deposit * the deposit and the third-party game bring-in ratio multiple; Calculation formula: the amount of third-party games that can be brought into = (club deposit - unsettled bills) * the margin and the proportion of third-party games brought into the game - the amount of third-party games that have been brought in = available margin * Margin and third-party games bring in proportional multipliers.

Example: A club has paid a deposit of 500, and the current unsettled bill is 100. The ratio between the deposit and the third-party game is 10. There are three members A, B and C. Among them, A has brought 100 balance to the third-party game. The current total amount that the club can bring into the third-party game is: ((500-100)*10)-100 = 3900, that is, members B and C can bring a maximum balance of 3900 to a third party for gaming in the future.

Example:

Assume that the commission ratio of the current net profit is 10% (that is, from the direct subordinates), and the extra commission for the cross-level part is an additional fixed bonus for the webmaster, and the bonus ratio is 30%. A was the first to discover business opportunities and immediately developed B1, B2 and B3; B1 further developed C1 and C2; B2 had no subordinates; B3 developed C3, and C1 developed D1. On the third day, B1's net profit is -500, B2's net profit is -3000, B3's net profit is 2000, C1's net profit is 1000, C2's net profit is -2000, and C3's net profit is -20 000, D1's net profit is -3000, of which A's last period balance is -600 (that is, the amount of the platform's loss at the last settlement), and the last period balances of B1, B3, and C1 are 0 (that is, the platform has no losses at the last settlement).

Then the income between them is calculated as follows:
  • 1. What is net profit? Net profit = member’s win or loss in this period + receiving rewards + recharge fee + withdrawal fee + three-party game cost
    • (1) Take B1 as an example: Suppose B1’s win or loss during the current settlement period is -700 (i.e. B1 Loss of 700), and during the period B1 received 100 rewards from the platform, B1's recharge cost the platform 15 handling fees, B1's withdrawal cost the platform 5 handling fees, and B1's entry into third-party games cost the platform 80 costs, then B1's net profit = -700+100+15+5+80 =-500, that is, B1 contributes 500 to A;
    • (2) Take B3 as an example: Assume that B3 wins or loses 1,900 during the current settlement period (that is, B3 makes a profit of 1,900), and during the period, B3 receives 100 from the platform As a reward, B3's recharge costs the platform 15 handling fees, B3's withdrawal costs the platform 5 handling fees, and B3's entry into third-party games costs the platform 80 costs. Then B3's net profit = 1900+100+15+5+80=2000, that is, B3's contribution to A is -2000.
  • 2. A’s income is calculated as follows:
    • (1) A’s explanation: In addition to the contributions of direct subordinates B1, B2 and B3, A’s commission sources also include contributions from other subordinates C1, C2, C3 and D1;
    • (2)A’s direct performance: That is, the performance contributed by A’s direct subordinates B1, B2 and B3 to A. The calculation formula is: direct performance =-(B1 net profit + B2 net profit + B3 net profit) =-(-500-3000+2000)=1 500;
    • (3) A’s performance for the current period: That is, the sum of A’s direct performance and the previous period’s balance. The calculation formula is: current period’s performance = direct performance + previous period’s balance = 1500 -600=900;
    • (4) A’s direct commission: Direct commission = current period’s performance * commission ratio = 900*10% = 90;
    • (5) A’s other commissions: That is, commissions contributed by C1, C2, C3 and D1, the calculation formula is: other commissions =-(C1 net profit + C2 net profit Profit + C3 net profit + D1 net profit) * rebate ratio * gift ratio = - (1000-2000-20000-3000) * 10% * 30% = 720;
    • (6)A’s commission for this period:Commission for this period = direct commission + other commissions = 90+720=810.
  • 3. B1’s income is calculated as follows:
    • (1) Description of B1: In addition to direct subordinates C1 and C2, B1’s commission sources also include contributions from other subordinates D1; ​​
    • (2) B1’s direct Subordinate performance: That is, the performance contributed by B1's direct subordinates C1 and C2. The calculation formula is: direct performance = - (C1 net profit + C2 net profit) = (-1000 + 2000) = 1000.00;
    • (3) B1's current performance: That is, the sum of B1's direct performance and the previous period's balance, the calculation formula is: current period performance = Direct performance + previous period balance = 1000 + 0 = 1000;
    • (4) B1’s direct commission: Direct commission = current period’s performance * rebate ratio = 1000*10% = 100;
    • (5) B1’s other commissions: That is, from D1’s contribution, The calculation formula is: other commissions = -D1 net profit * rebate ratio * gift ratio = 3000 * 10% * 30% = 90;
    • (6) B1’s commission for this period: Commission for this period = direct commission + other commissions = 100 + 90 = 190.
  • 4. B2’s income is calculated as follows:
    • (1) Description of B2: Because B2 has no subordinates, B2 has no commission, that is, B2’s current commission = 0.
  • 5. B3’s income is calculated as follows:
    • (1) Description of B1: Because B3 has only one direct subordinate C3, B3’s commission comes from the contribution of its direct subordinate C3;
    • (2)B3’s direct performance:That is, the contribution performance of B3’s direct subordinate C3, the calculation formula is: direct performance=-C3 net profit=20,000;
    • (3)B3’s current performance:That is, the sum of B3’s direct performance and the previous period’s performance, the calculation formula is: current performance=direct industry Performance + last period balance = 20000 + 0 = 20000;
    • (4) B3’s direct commission: Direct commission = current period’s performance * rebate ratio = 20000*10% = 2000;
    • (5) B3’s other commissions: ng>Because C3 has not developed subordinates, B3 has no other commissions, that is, other commissions = 0;
    • (6) B3’s commission for this period: Commission for this period = direct commission + other commissions = 2000+0=2000.
  • 6. C1’s income is calculated as follows:
    • (1) Description of C1: Because C1 has only one direct subordinate D1, C1’s commission comes from the contribution of its direct subordinate D1;
    • (2)C1’s direct performance:That is, the contribution performance of C1’s direct subordinate D1. The calculation formula is: direct performance=-D1 net profit=3000;
    • (3)C1’s current performance:That is the sum of C1’s direct performance and the previous period’s performance. The calculation formula is: current performance=direct performance Subordinate performance + balance of the previous period = 3000 + 0 = 3000;
    • (4) Direct commission of C1: Direct commission = performance of the current period * rebate ratio = 3000*10% = 300;
    • (5) Other commissions of C1: ng>Because D1 has not developed subordinates, C1 has no other commissions, that is, other commissions = 0;
    • (6) C1’s commission for this period: Commission for this period = direct commission + other commissions = 300 + 0 = 300.

Example:

Assume the current rebate for valid bets is 10% (from direct subordinates), with an additional 30% bonus for cross-level commissions. A recruits B1, B2, and B3; B1 recruits C1 and C2; B2 has no subordinates; B3 recruits C3. On the second day, the valid bets are: B1 (500), B2 (3000), B3 (2000), C1 (1000), C2 (2000), and C3 (20000). Earnings calculation:

Earnings calculation:
  • 1. A's earnings:
    • (1) Source: A earns from direct subordinates (B1, B2, and B3) and indirect subordinates (C1, C2, and C3).
    • (2) A’s direct performance: From performances of A’s direct subordinates (B1, B2 and B3). Calculation: A’s direct performance = B1’s valid bet + B2’s valid bet + B3’s valid bet = 500 + 3000 + 2000 = 5500.
    • (3) A’s direct commission = A’s direct performance x Commission rate = 5500 x 10% = 550.
    • (4) A’s indirect commission: From performances of A’s indirect subordinates (C1, C2, C3 and D1). Calculation: A’s indirect commission = (C1’s valid bet + C2’s valid bet + C3’s valid bet) x Commission rate x Bonus rate = (1000 + 2000 + 20000) x 10% x 30% = 690.
    • (5) A’s current commission = A’s direct commission + A’s indirect commission = 550 + 690 = 1240.
  • 2. B1’s earnings: (No subordinate) = (0 + 0) x 1% = 0
    • (1) Source: B1 earns from direct subordinates (C1 and C2).
    • (2) B1’s direct performance: From performances of B1’s direct subordinates (C1 and C2). Calculation: B1’s direct performance = C1’s valid bet + C2’s valid bet = 1000 + 2000 = 3000.
    • (3) B1’s direct commission = B1’s direct performance x Commission rate = 3000 x 10% = 300.
    • (4) B1’s indirect commission = 0 (since C1 and C2 have no subordinate).
    • (5) B1’s current commission = B1’s direct commission + B1’s indirect commission = 300 + 0 = 300.
  • 3. B2’s earnings:
    • (1) B2’s commission = 0 (since B2 has no subordinate).
  • 4. B3’s earnings: Besides contributions from direct subordinates (B1, B2, and B3), there are also contributions from indirect subordinates (C1, C2, and C3):
    • (1) Source: B3 earns from a direct subordinate (C3).
    • (2) B3’s direct performance: From performances of B3’s direct subordinate (C3). Calculation: B3’s direct performance = C3’s valid bet = 20000.
    • (3) B3’s direct commission = B3’s direct performance x Commission rate = 20000 x 10% = 2000.
    • (4) B3’s indirect commission = 0 (since C3 has no subordinate).
    • (5) B3’s current commission = B3’s direct commission + B3’s indirect commission = 2000 + 0 = 2000.
  • 5. Notes:

    National agents allow unlimited horizontal and vertical growth. For simplicity, this example uses a 2-level structure.

Refers to the maximum amount that a single member of the club can carry into third-party games (including WG Sports) in the future (excluding WG native games and club games).

Important Tips:1. The bigger the limit for a single member to enter third-party games, the better. It is based on the actual needs of the club to prevent a single member from occupying the entire club's three-party game quota, affecting the experience of other members. 2. After members finish playing third-party games, they must be reminded to return to the lobby normally to release their quota, so as to avoid abnormal exits midway and continue to occupy the quota.

For example: The current balance of a member of Club A is 1,000, and the member’s maximum bring-in of three-party games is 600 (assuming that the club has sufficient available margin), and the ratio of the margin to third-party games is 10 , then member A can only bring up to 600 to third-party games (the remaining 400 is left in the lobby); if the club currently has a available deposit of 30 (that is, the entire club can only bring 300 into third-party games), then member A can only bring up to 300 to third-party games (the remaining 700 is left in the lobby).

Example:

Suppose that the current valid bets from 0 to 10,000 are rebates of 100 (i.e. 1%) for every 10,000 (i.e. 1%), and the valid bets above 10,000 are rebates of 300 (i.e. 3%) for every 10,000 (i.e. 3%). A is the first to discover business opportunities on this site and immediately develops B1, B2 and B3, B1 C1 and C2 are further developed, B2 has no subordinates, and B3 develops the relatively powerful C3. The next day, B1's effective bet is 500, B2's effective bet is 3,000, B3's effective bet is 2,000, C1's effective bet is 1,000, C2's effective bet is 2,000, and C3's effective bet reaches 20,000.

Then the income calculation method between them is as follows:
  • 1. What is the extreme rebate ratio?

    The extreme rebate ratio refers to: In the settlement of this period, the difference between the rebate ratio corresponding to the total performance of the current agent and the rebate ratio corresponding to the total performance of his direct subordinates is called the extreme rebate ratio.

    For example: Suppose A's total performance is 28,000, then the corresponding rebate ratio is 3%; the direct subordinate B1's total performance is 3,000, then the corresponding rebate ratio is 1%; the direct subordinate B3's total performance is 20,000, then the corresponding rebate ratio is 3%. According to calculations, the extreme rebate ratio of A and B1 is: 3%-1%=2%; the extreme rebate ratio of A and B3 is: 3%-3%=0%.

  • 2. B1’s income is calculated as follows:
    • (1) B1 description: B1’s commission comes from the contribution of its direct subordinates C1 and C2;
    • (2) B1’s direct performance: That is, the performance contributed by B1’s direct subordinates C1 and C2 to A, The calculation formula is: direct performance = C1 effective bet + C2 effective bet = 1000 + 2000 = 3000;
    • (3) B1’s other performance: Because B1’s direct subordinates C1 and C2 have no subordinates, B1’s other performance is 0;
    • (4) B1’s total performance: Total performance = direct performance + other Performance = 3000+0=3000;
    • (5)B1’s rebate ratio:Because B1’s total performance is 3,000, the corresponding rebate ratio is 1%;
    • (6)B1’s direct commission:Direct commission = direct performance * rebate ratio = 3000*1%=30;
    • (7) B1’s other commissions: Since C1 and C2 have no subordinates, B2 has no other commissions, that is, other commissions = 0;
    • (8) B1’s commissions for this period: Commissions for this period = direct commissions + other commissions = 30 + 0 = 30.
  • 3. B2’s income is calculated as follows:
    • (1) Description of B2: Because B2 has no subordinates, B2 has no commission, that is, B2’s current commission = 0.
  • 4. B3’s income method is as follows:
    • (1) B3 description: B3’s commission comes from the contribution of its direct subordinate C3;
    • (2) B3’s direct performance: That is, the performance contributed by B3’s direct subordinate C3 to B3 , the calculation formula is: direct performance = C3 effective bet = 20000 = 20000;
    • (3) B3’s other performance: Because B3’s direct subordinate C3 has no development subordinates, B1’s other performance is 0;
    • (4) B3’s total performance: Total performance = direct performance + other performance = 20000+0 =20000;
    • (5)B3’s rebate ratio:Because B3’s total performance is 20,000, the corresponding rebate ratio is 3%;
    • (6)B3’s direct commission:Direct commission = direct performance * rebate ratio = 20000*3%=600;
    • (7) B3’s other commissions: Since C3 has no subordinates, B3 has no other commissions; that is, other commissions = 0;
    • (8) B3’s commissions for this period: Commissions for this period = direct commissions + other commissions = 600 + 0 = 600.
  • 5. A’s income is calculated as follows:
    • (1) A’s explanation: In addition to the contributions of direct subordinates B1, B2 and B3, A’s commission sources also come from other subordinates C1, C2 and C3;
    • (2) A’s direct performance: That is, A’s direct subordinates B1 and B2 And the performance contributed by B3 to A, the calculation formula is: direct performance = B1 effective bet + B2 effective bet + B3 effective bet = 500 + 3000 + 2000 = 5500;
    • (3) Other of A Other performance:That is, the contribution of A’s other subordinates C1, C2 and C3. The calculation formula is: other performance = C1 effective bet + C2 effective bet + C3 effective bet = 1000 + 2000 + 20000 = 23000;
    • (4)A’s total performance:Total performance = direct performance + other performance = 5500+23000=28500;
    • (5) A’s rebate ratio: Since A’s total performance is 20,000, A’s rebate ratio is 3%;
    • (6) A’s direct commission: Direct commission = direct industry Performance * commission ratio = 5500 * 3% = 165;
    • (7) A’s other commissions: Because the commission ratio of A’s direct subordinate B1 is 1%, so A The extreme difference between A and B1 is 3%-1%=2%; because the direct subordinate B2 has no total performance and therefore has no rebate ratio, there is no extreme difference in commission ratio; because the immediate subordinate B3’s rebate ratio is 3%, the extreme difference between A and B3 is 3%-3%=0%. The calculation formula is: other commissions = B1’s total performance * extremely poor rebate ratio + B2’s total performance * extremely poor rebate ratio + B3’s total performance * extremely poor rebate ratio = 3000*2%+0+20000*0%=60;
    • (8) A’s commission for this period: Commission for this period = direct commission + other commissions = 165 + 60 = 225.
  • 6. Infinitus additional explanation:

    Because the infinitely different agency model can develop infinite subordinate levels, that is, N represents infinite levels, it can develop both horizontally and vertically; for the convenience of explanation, this article only takes a 2-layer structure as an example.

Amount brought into third-party games refers to the total funds brought by all club members into third-party games (excluding WG native games and club games). Formula: Amount brought into third-party games = A member's balance + B member's balance + ... + N member's balance.

Example: A club has members A, B, and C. A brings 100, and B brings 300 into third-party games. The total amount brought into third-party games is: 100 + 300 = 400.

What is the Club Guarantee? Because third-party games (including WG Sports) need to pay bills in proportion on a regular basis. In order to prevent bad debts or malicious consumption, the club needs to pay a certain deposit before it can be opened. The deposit determines the bill amount and the amount of third-party games that can be brought in (except for WG native games and club games, which are not subject to the limit). Therefore, the more deposits paid, the greater the amount that can be brought into third-party games; and the corresponding deposit will be automatically deducted after the bill is settled, so a deposit must be recharged from time to time to ensure the normal operation of the club. Calculation formula: Club deposit = unsettled bills + (three parties that can be brought in + three parties that have been brought in) / 10.

For example: A club has paid a deposit of 5,000, and the current unsettled bill is 1,000. The ratio of the deposit and the third-party game brought in is 10. Then the third-party game has been brought in and the maximum amount that can be brought into the third-party game is: (5000-1000)*10 = 40000.

Refers to the current club deposit after deducting unsettled bills and the amount of deposit remaining in third-party games (including WG Sports) (which can be withdrawn directly). The available margin determines the total amount of third-party games that members can bring into the future, so the greater the available margin, the greater the amount that members can bring into third-party games. Calculation formula: Available deposit = Club deposit - Unsettled bills - Third-party brought-in/margin and third-party game brought-in ratio multiple = Three-party game limit that can be brought in/margin and third-party game brought-in ratio multiple.

For example: A club has paid a deposit of 500. The current unsettled bill is 100. The ratio between the deposit and the third-party game is 10. There are three members A, B and C. Among them, A has brought a balance of 400 to the third-party game. The current available deposit for the club is: 500-100 - (400/10) = 360, that is, the current limit of three-party games that the club can bring into is: 360*10=3600.
Refers to total club's unpaid and pending bills.

The quota of third-party games that can be brought in refers to the sum of the quota of third-party games that can be brought into the future by members of the club who have not entered third-party games (including WG Sports) (excluding WG native games and club games), that is, the available deposit * the deposit and the third-party game bring-in ratio multiple; Calculation formula: the amount of third-party games that can be brought into = (club deposit - unsettled bills) * the margin and the proportion of third-party games brought into the game - the quota of third-party games = Available Margin * Margin and third party games bring in proportional multipliers.

For example: A club has paid a deposit of 500, and the current unsettled bill is 100. The ratio between the deposit and the third-party game brought in is 10. There are three members A, B and C. Among them, A has brought a balance of 100 to the third-party game. The total amount that the current club can bring into the third-party game is: ((500-100)*10)-100 = 3900, that is, members B and C can bring a maximum balance of 3900 to a third party for gaming in the future.

Refers to the maximum amount that a single member of the club can carry into third-party games (including WG Sports) in the future (excluding WG native games and club games).

Refers to the current club deposit after deducting unsettled bills and the amount of deposit remaining in third-party games (including WG Sports) (which can be withdrawn directly). The available margin determines the total amount of third-party games that members can bring into the future, so the greater the available margin, the greater the amount that members can bring into third-party games. Calculation formula: Available deposit = Club deposit - Unsettled bills - Third-party brought-in/margin and third-party game brought-in ratio multiple = Three-party game limit that can be brought in/margin and third-party game brought-in ratio multiple.

For example: A club has paid a deposit of 500. The current unsettled bill is 100. The ratio between the deposit and the third-party game is 10. There are three members A, B and C. Among them, A has brought a balance of 400 to the third-party game. The current available deposit for the club is: 500-100 - (400/10) = 360, that is, the current limit of three-party games that the club can bring into is: 360*10=3600.

Since both recharges and bets receive real-time commission rebates starting from the lowest level, the lowest commission ratio is calculated at the beginning of the settlement cycle. As performance accumulates, the ratio gradually increases, and towards the end of the settlement period, the commission ratio becomes higher. And after the settlement cycle ends, the system will compensate the difference in one go according to the highest rebate ratio. The calculation formula of the difference is: Period difference = period performance x highest rebate ratio in the period - the period has been collected.

Take the smallest value as the valid bet:

The valid bet amount is the smaller value between the bet amount and the absolute value of the win/loss. For example, with odds of 0.20 and 1.50: D bets 500.00 at odds of 0.20, with a win of 100.00, so D's valid bet is 100.00 (using the win/loss amount). E bets 500.00 at odds of 1.50, with a win of 750.00, so E's valid bet is 500.00 (using the bet amount).
